one_line_ask: Refine my ChatGPT-made "Vanguard" athletic badge into clean, separated spot-color screen-print artwork (olive drab / khaki / black / dark grey + digital camo), vectorize it, and deliver it in multiple file formats with spot-color info.
brief: I run a youth/athletic "Vanguard" team and I already mocked up our logo set in ChatGPT, but the files are messy raster PNGs I can't actually use. I need them refined and turned into real production artwork I can hand to a screen printer and also post on social. The combined badge has several elements — the "VANGUARD" wordmark (UC), a subdued American flag, a star icon, chevrons, a soldier head mark, and the outer badge ring. For screen printing each of these has to print as a FLAT spot color, so I need every element reduced to a single clean ink from my military palette — olive drab, khaki, black, and dark grey — with the digital-camo swatch I made available as a fill/accent. Right now the ChatGPT export is dull, slightly crooked, has anti-aliased fuzzy color edges, and the colors aren't consistent enough to separate. So: straighten and tidy it, auto-tone/level it so the palette reads true, then go element-by-element and lock each named mark to one solid spot ink (so my printer gets clean separations), pull a true black plate and a knockout, vectorize the cleaned art into scalable vector, and give me a digital-camo accent swatch as a separate vector too. Final delivery: a genuine l
